Option B: Announce deprecation of the old version of the API. Introduce the new version of the API. Contact remaining users on the old API. Deprecate the old version of the API. Turn down the old version of the API. Provide best effort support to users of the old API.
Option C: Announce deprecation of the old version of the API. Contact remaining users on the old API. Introduce the new version of the API. Deprecate the old version of the API. Provide best effort support to users of the old API. Turn down the old version of the API.
Option D: Introduce the new version of the API. Contact remaining users of the old API. Announce deprecation of the old version of the API. Deprecate the old version of the API. Turn down the old version of the API. Provide best effort support to users of the old API.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Use the filter-record-transformer Fluentd filter plugin to remove the fields from the log entries in flight.
Option B: Use the fluent-plugin-record-reformer Fluentd output plugin to remove the fields from the log entries in flight.
Option C: Wait for the application developers to patch the application, and then verify that the log entries are no longer exposing PII.
Option D: Stage log entries to Cloud Storage, and then trigger a Cloud Function to remove the fields and write the entries to Stackdriver via the Stackdriver Logging API.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Focus on developing new features rather than avoiding the outages from recurring.
Option B: Focus on identifying the contributing causes of the incident rather than the individual responsible for the cause.
Option C: Plan individual meetings with all the engineers involved. Determine who approved and pushed the new release to production.
Option D: Use the Git history to find the related code commit. Prevent the engineer who made that commit from working on production services.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Add more serving capacity to all of your application's zones.
Option B: Have more frequent or potentially risky application releases.
Option C: Tighten the SLO match the application's observed reliability.
Option D: Implement and measure additional Service Level Indicators (SLIs) fro the application.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Make the service's SLO more strict.
Option B: Increase the service's deployment velocity and/or risk.
Option C: Shift engineering time to other services that need more reliability.
Option D: Get the product team to prioritize reliability work over new features.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Identify engineers responsible for the incident and escalate to the senior management.
Option B: Ensure that test cases that catch errors of this type are run successfully before new software releases.
Option C: Follow up with the employees who reviewed the changes and prescribe practices they should follow in the future.
Option D: Design a policy that will require on-call teams to immediately call engineers and management to discuss a plan of action if an incident occurs.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Replace the CAB with a senior manager to ensure continuous oversight from development to deployment.
Option B: Let developers merge their own changes, but ensure that the team's deployment platform can roll back changes if any issues are discovered.
Option C: Move to a peer-review based process for individual changes that is enforced at code check-in time and supported by automated tests.
Option D: Batch changes into larger but less frequent software releases.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Deploy the canary release of the application to Cloud Run. Use traffic splitting to direct 10% of user traffic to the canary release based on the revision tag.
Option B: Deploy the canary release of the application to App Engine. Use traffic splitting to direct a subset of user traffic to the new version based on the IP address.
Option C: Deploy the canary release of the application to Compute Engine. Use Anthos Service Mesh with Compute Engine to direct 10% of user traffic to the canary release by configuring the virtual service.
Option D: Deploy the canary release to Google Kubernetes Engine with Anthos Service Mesh. Use traffic splitting to direct 10% of user traffic to the new version based on the user-agent header configured in the virtual service.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Create SLIs from metrics. Enable Alert Policies if the services do not pass.
Option B: Use the metrics from Anthos Service Mesh to measure the health of the microservices.
Option C: Create a SLO. Create an Alert Policy on select_slo_burn_rate.
Option D: Create a SLO and configure uptime checks for your services. Enable Alert Policies if the services do not pass.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Use the gcloud CLI to install the Ops Agent on each VM listed in the Cloud Asset Inventory,
Option B: Select all VMs with an Agent status of Not detected on the Cloud Operations VMs dashboard. Then select Install agents.
Option C: Use the gcloud CLI to create an Agent Policy.
Option D: Install the Ops Agent on the Compute Engine image by using a startup script

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Enable the Workflows API and route all the logs to Cloud Logging.
Option B: Create a central Cloud Monitoring workspace and attach all related projects.
Option C: Create an aggregated log sink associated with the production folder that uses a Pub/Sub topic as the destination.
Option D: Create an aggregated log sink associated with the production folder that uses a Cloud Logging bucket as the destination.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Premium Tier with a global load balancer
Option B: Premium Tier with a regional load balancer
Option C: Standard Tier with a global load balancer
Option D: Standard Tier with a regional load balancer

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Perform a rolling deployment, and test your new application after the deployment is complete.
Option B: Perform A/B testing, and test your application periodically after the deployment is complete.
Option C: Perform a canary deployment, and test your new application periodically after the new version is deployed.
Option D: Perform a blue/green deployment, and test your new application after the deployment is complete.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Deploy the new version of the service to the staging environment. Split the traffic, and allow 1% of traffic through to the latest version. Test the latest version. If the test passes, gradually roll out the latest version to the staging and production environments.
Option B: Deploy the new version of the service to the staging environment. Split the traffic, and allow 50% of traffic through to the latest version. Test the latest version. If the test passes, send all traffic to the latest version. Repeat for the production environment.
Option C: Deploy the new version of the service to the staging environment with a new-release tag without serving traffic. Test the new-release version. If the test passes, gradually roll out this tagged version. Repeat for the production environment.
Option D: Deploy a new environment with the green tag to use as the staging environment. Deploy the new version of the service to the green environment and test the new version. If the tests pass, send all traffic to the green environment and delete the existing staging environment. Repeat for the production environment.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Add N+1 redundancy to your service by adding additional compute resources to the service.
Option B: Identify, measure, and eliminate toil by automating repetitive tasks.
Option C: Define an error budget for your service level availability and minimize the remaining error budget.
Option D: Allocate available engineers to the feature backlog while you ensure that the service remains within the availability target.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Recreate deployment and canary testing
Option B: Blue/green deployment and canary testing
Option C: Rolling update deployment and A/B testing
Option D: Rolling update deployment and shadow testing

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Create a new Kubernetes service account, and assign the service account to the Pods. Use Workload Identity to authenticate as the Google service account.
Option B: Create a new JSON service account key for the Google service account, store the key as a Kubernetes secret, inject the key into the Pods, and set the GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS environment variable.
Option C: Create a new Google service account, and assign the appropriate IAM permissions.
Option D: Create a new JSON service account key for the Google service account, store the key in the secret management store for the CI/CD tool, and configure Terraform to use this key for authentication.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Decrease the acknowledgment deadline on the subscription.
Option B: Add a virtual queue to the online store that allows typical traffic levels.
Option C: Increase the number of Pod replicas.
Option D: Increase the Pod CPU and memory limits.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Use Google Cloud Deploy to deploy the network policies and the DaemonSet. Use Cloud Monitoring to trigger an alert if the network policies and DaemonSet drift from your source in the repository.
Option B: Use Google Cloud Deploy to deploy the DaemonSet and use Policy Controller to configure the network policies. Use Cloud Monitoring to detect drifts from the source in the repository and Cloud Functions to correct the drifts.
Option C: Use Cloud Build to render and deploy the network policies and the DaemonSet. Set up Config Sync to sync the configurations for the three environments.
Option D: Use Cloud Build to render and deploy the network policies and the DaemonSet. Set up a Policy Controller to enforce the configurations for the three environments.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Create a new pipeline to delete old infrastructure stacks when they are no longer needed.
Option B: Confirm that the pipeline is storing and retrieving the terraform.tfstate file from Cloud Storage with the Terraform gcs backend.
Option C: Verify that the pipeline is storing and retrieving the terraform.tfstate file from a source control.
Option D: Update the pipeline to remove any existing infrastructure before you apply the latest configuration.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Create a single aggregated log sink at the organization level.
Option B: Create a log sink in each project.
Option C: Create two aggregated log sinks at the organization level, and filter by project ID.
Option D: Create an aggregated log sink in the Dev and Prod folders.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Configure Prometheus cross-service federation for centralized data access.
Option B: Configure workload metrics within Cloud Operations for GKE.
Option C: Configure Prometheus hierarchical federation for centralized data access.
Option D: Configure Google Cloud Managed Service for Prometheus.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: 1. Install a Git hook to require developers to run unit tests before pushing the code to a central repository.
Option B: 1. Install a Git hook to require developers to run unit tests before pushing the code to a central repository. If all tests are successful, build a container.
Option C: 1. Trigger Cloud Build to build the application container, and run unit tests with the container.
Option D: 1. Trigger Cloud Build to run unit tests when the code is pushed. If all unit tests are successful, build and push the application container to a central registry.

Question: Topic 1
Option A: Deploy the application through a continuous delivery pipeline by using canary deployments. Use Cloud Monitoring to look for performance issues, and ramp up traffic as supported by the metrics.
Option B: Deploy the application through a continuous delivery pipeline by using blue/green deployments. Migrate traffic to the new version of the application and use Cloud Monitoring to look for performance issues.
Option C: Deploy the application by using kubectl and use Config Connector to slowly ramp up traffic between versions. Use Cloud Monitoring to look for performance issues.
Option D: Deploy the application by using kubectl and set the spec.updateStrategy.type field to RollingUpdate. Use Cloud Monitoring to look for performance issues, and run the kubectl rollback command if there are any issues.
